title: Dataset Open Question P
rewritten_question_text: A block of mass \(m_1 = 1\,\mathrm{kg}\) and another mass \(m_2 = 2\,\mathrm{kg}\) are placed together on an inclined plane. The coefficient of friction between \(m_1\) and the plane is zero, and the coefficients of static and kinetic friction between \(m_2\) and the plane are both \(\mu = 0.3\). What is the friction on block \(m_2\) when \(\theta = 5^\circ\)?

title: Dataset Open Question Q
rewritten_question_text: A block of mass \(m_1 = 1\,\mathrm{kg}\) and another mass \(m_2 = 2\,\mathrm{kg}\) are placed together on an inclined plane. The coefficient of friction between \(m_1\) and the plane is zero, and the coefficients of static and kinetic friction between \(m_2\) and the plane are both \(\mu = 0.3\). What is the friction on block \(m_2\) when \(\theta = 10^\circ\)?

title: Dataset Open Question R
rewritten_question_text: A block of mass \(m_1 = 1\,\mathrm{kg}\) and another mass \(m_2 = 2\,\mathrm{kg}\) are placed together on an inclined plane. The coefficient of friction between \(m_1\) and the plane is zero, and the coefficients of static and kinetic friction between \(m_2\) and the plane are both \(\mu = 0.3\). What is the friction on block \(m_2\) when \(\theta = 15^\circ\)?

title: Dataset Open Question S
rewritten_question_text: A block of mass \(m_1 = 1\,\mathrm{kg}\) and another mass \(m_2 = 2\,\mathrm{kg}\) are placed together on an inclined plane. The coefficient of friction between \(m_1\) and the plane is zero, and the coefficients of static and kinetic friction between \(m_2\) and the plane are both \(\mu = 0.3\). What is the friction on block \(m_2\) when \(\theta = 20^\circ\)?

title: Image Point x-Coordinate
rewritten_question_text:
A small object is placed 50 cm to the left of a thin convex lens of focal length 30 cm. A convex spherical mirror of radius of curvature 100 cm is placed to the right of the lens at a distance of 50 cm. The mirror is tilted such that the axis of the mirror is at an angle 30° to the axis of the lens, as shown in the figure.

If the origin of the coordinate system is taken to be at the centre of the lens, what is the x-coordinate (in cm) of the point at which the image is formed?

title: Image Point y-Coordinate
rewritten_question_text:
A small object is placed 50 cm to the left of a thin convex lens of focal length 30 cm. A convex spherical mirror of radius of curvature 100 cm is placed to the right of the lens at a distance of 50 cm. The mirror is tilted such that the axis of the mirror is at an angle 30° to the axis of the lens, as shown in the figure.

If the origin of the coordinate system is taken to be at the centre of the lens, what is the y-coordinate (in cm) of the point at which the image is formed?

title: Dataset Open Question
rewritten_question_text: Two identical glass rods $S_1$ and $S_2$ (refractive index = 1.5) have one convex end of radius of curvature 10 cm. They are placed with the curved surfaces at a distance $d$ as shown in the figure, with their axes (shown by the dashed line) aligned. When a point source of light $P$ is placed inside rod $S_1$ on its axis at a distance of 50 cm from the curved face, the light rays emanating from it are found to be parallel to the axis inside $S_2$. What is the distance $d$?

title: Dataset Open Question
rewritten_question_text: After the material of permittivity $\epsilon$ and conductivity $\sigma$ is introduced at time $t=0$, how does the magnitude of the current density $j(t)$ at any fixed point in the material vary with time?

title: Dataset Open Question
rewritten_question_text: An infinitely long hollow conducting cylinder with inner radius $R/2$ and outer radius $R$ carries a uniform current density along its length. What is the piecewise dependence of the magnetic-field magnitude $|\vec{B}|$ on the radial distance $r$ from the axis?

title: Dataset Open Question
rewritten_question_text: In the figure, three resistor configurations R1, R2 and R3 are connected to a 3 V battery. If the power dissipated by the configurations is P1, P2 and P3 respectively, arrange P1, P2 and P3 in decreasing order.

title: Dataset Open Question
rewritten_question_text: A bob of mass M is suspended by a massless string of length L. The horizontal velocity V at position A is just sufficient to make it reach the point B. For the angle $\theta$ at which the speed of the bob is half of that at A, what range does $\theta$ lie in?

title: Dataset Open Question - Direction of $I_1$
rewritten_question_text: The figure shows certain wire segments joined together to form a coplanar loop. The loop is placed in a perpendicular magnetic field directed into the plane of the figure, and the magnitude of the field increases with time. In which direction does the current $I_1$ flow in segment ab?

The loop is placed in a perpendicular magnetic field directed into the plane of the figure, and the magnitude of the field increases with time. In which direction does the current $I_2$ flow in segment cd?

title: Force direction for configuration P
rewritten_question_text: Four charges of equal magnitude are fixed on the x-axis at x = -2a, -a, +a, and +2a as Q1, Q2, Q3, and Q4, respectively. A positive charge q is placed on the positive y-axis at a distance b > 0 from the origin. If Q1, Q2, Q3, and Q4 are all positive, what is the direction of the net force on q?

title: Force direction for configuration Q
rewritten_question_text: Four charges of equal magnitude are fixed on the x-axis at x = -2a, -a, +a, and +2a as Q1, Q2, Q3, and Q4, respectively. A positive charge q is placed on the positive y-axis at a distance b > 0 from the origin. If Q1 and Q2 are positive while Q3 and Q4 are negative, what is the direction of the net force on q?

title: Force direction for configuration R
rewritten_question_text: Four charges of equal magnitude are fixed on the x-axis at x = -2a, -a, +a, and +2a as Q1, Q2, Q3, and Q4, respectively. A positive charge q is placed on the positive y-axis at a distance b > 0 from the origin. If Q1 and Q4 are positive while Q2 and Q3 are negative, what is the direction of the net force on q?

title: Force direction for configuration S
rewritten_question_text: Four charges of equal magnitude are fixed on the x-axis at x = -2a, -a, +a, and +2a as Q1, Q2, Q3, and Q4, respectively. A positive charge q is placed on the positive y-axis at a distance b > 0 from the origin. If Q1 and Q3 are positive while Q2 and Q4 are negative, what is the direction of the net force on q?

title: Measured Value by Caliper C1
rewritten_question_text: There are two Vernier calipers, both of which have 1 cm divided into 10 equal divisions on the main scale. The Vernier scale of caliper C1 has 10 equal divisions corresponding to 9 main scale divisions. The readings of the calipers are shown in the figure. What is the measured value by caliper C1, in cm?

title: Measured Value by Caliper C2
rewritten_question_text: There are two Vernier calipers, both of which have 1 cm divided into 10 equal divisions on the main scale. The Vernier scale of caliper C2 has 10 equal divisions corresponding to 11 main scale divisions. The readings of the calipers are shown in the figure. What is the measured value by caliper C2, in cm?

title: Dataset Open Question
rewritten_question_text: A billiard ball rolling across a table in the \( +x \) direction at \( 3 \, \text{m/s} \) hits the edge of the table at a perpendicular angle, and bounces back in the \( -x \) direction, now traveling at \( 2 \, \text{m/s} \) in the opposite direction. The greatest magnitude of acceleration for the billiard ball was at what time?

title: Dataset Open Question 1
rewritten_question_text: At time \( t = 0 \), a red car and a blue car are both located at \( x = 0 \), with the red car travelling at a constant speed \( v \) along the positive \( x \)-axis and the blue car at rest and just beginning to accelerate along a path parallel to the red car. The velocity of both cars from time \( 0 \) to \( 2t \) is graphed above. At time \( t \), how do the distances traveled by the two cars compare?
expected_answer: both cars have traveled the same distance

title: Dataset Open Question 2
rewritten_question_text: At time \( t = 0 \), a red car and a blue car are both located at \( x = 0 \), with the red car travelling at a constant speed \( v \) along the positive \( x \)-axis and the blue car at rest and just beginning to accelerate along a path parallel to the red car. The velocity of both cars from time \( 0 \) to \( 2t \) is graphed above. At time \( t \), which car has the greater instantaneous velocity?
expected_answer: the blue car has a greater instantaneous velocity

title: Dataset Open Question
rewritten_question_text: The free-body diagram shows all forces acting on a box supported by a stationary horizontal surface, where the length of each force vector is proportional to its magnitude. Based on the diagram, in which direction must the box be moving?
expected_answer: The box must be moving to the right, as indicated by the Force of friction in the opposite direction.

title: Dataset Open Question
rewritten_question_text: A billiard ball hits the side of a pool table at an angle \(\Theta\) and bounces away at the same angle with the same speed. In what direction is the net change in momentum of the billiard ball?

title: Electric Field x-Component Direction
rewritten_question_text: A conductor lying in the \(xy\) plane is exposed to a magnetic field in the \(+z\) direction, and connected to a source of potential as shown above. In which x-direction does the electric field within the conductor point?

title: Electric Field y-Component Direction
rewritten_question_text: A conductor lying in the \(xy\) plane is exposed to a magnetic field in the \(+z\) direction, and connected to a source of potential as shown above. In which y-direction does the electric field within the conductor point?

title: Dataset Open Question
rewritten_question_text: Two long parallel wires, oriented along the \( y \)-axis as shown, contain currents \( I \) and \( 2I \), flowing in opposite directions. In what direction is the force on wire \( 2I \)?
expected_answer: The force on wire \( 2I \) is in the \( +x \) direction.

title: Dataset Open Question
rewritten_question_text: A square loop of wire, with sides of length \( L \), is oriented in the \( x \)-\( y \) plane and able to rotate about an axle along the \( y \)-axis running through the middle of the loop, as shown. The loop carries a current \( I \) in the direction indicated. In which direction should a magnetic field \( B \) be oriented so as to produce a clockwise torque on the current loop?

title: Dataset Open Question
rewritten_question_text:
A long bar magnet is placed above a current loop oriented as shown. What is the direction of the force on the North pole of the bar magnet due to the current loop?

title: Dataset Open Question - Magnitude
rewritten_question_text: Four charges are placed at the corners of a square with sides of length \(d\) as shown. What is the magnitude of the electric field at point \(X\), the center of the square?

title: Dataset Open Question - Direction
rewritten_question_text: Four charges are placed at the corners of a square with sides of length \(d\) as shown. In which direction does the electric field at point \(X\), the center of the square, point?

title: Dataset Open Question 1
rewritten_question_text: A light, rigid, insulating rod of length \( L \) is free to rotate vertically about a horizontal axis at one end. At the other end of the rod, a charged sphere of mass \( m \) and charge \( -Q \) is mounted. The rod is released in a horizontal position while an electric field is applied in the vicinity of the sphere. What magnitude of electric field is required to maintain the sphere-rod in the horizontal orientation?

title: Dataset Open Question 2
rewritten_question_text: A light, rigid, insulating rod of length \( L \) is free to rotate vertically about a horizontal axis at one end. At the other end of the rod, a charged sphere of mass \( m \) and charge \( -Q \) is mounted. The rod is released in a horizontal position while an electric field is applied in the vicinity of the sphere. In what direction must the electric field point to maintain the sphere-rod in the horizontal orientation?

title: EMMA-Physics Open Question
rewritten_question_text: An uncharged asymmetrically shaped conducting shell contains a point charge \(+q\). What is the net electric flux through the shell?
expected_answer: The electric flux through the asymmetric shell is \( \frac{q}{\epsilon_0} \).

title: Dataset Open Question
rewritten_question_text: A non-conducting sphere of uniformly distributed charge \(+Q\) is surrounded by a conducting shell with no net charge. What false statement can be made about the electric potential inside the non-conducting sphere?
expected_answer: All points in the non-conducting sphere are at the same potential.

title: Center of Mass x-Coordinate
rewritten_question_text: A rectangular metal frame, with height \(2L\) and width \(4L\), has a \(1L\)-by-\(1L\) corner section removed, as shown. What is the \(x\)-coordinate of the center of mass, in units of \(L\)?

title: Center of Mass y-Coordinate
rewritten_question_text: A rectangular metal frame, with height \(2L\) and width \(4L\), has a \(1L\)-by-\(1L\) corner section removed, as shown. What is the \(y\)-coordinate of the center of mass, in units of \(L\)?

title: Dataset Open Question
rewritten_question_text:
A large Ferris wheel at an amusement park has four seats, located \(90^\circ\) from each other and at a distance \(R\) from the axis. Each seat is attached to the wheel by a strong axle. As the Ferris wheel rotates with a constant angular velocity \(\omega\), the seats move past positions \(A\), \(B\), \(C\), and \(D\) as shown.

What force must an axle provide to keep a seat of mass \(m\) moving past position \(A\)? Express your answer in terms of \(m\), \(g\), \(R\), and \(\omega\).

title: Dataset Open Question
rewritten_question_text: A ride at an amusement park consists of a hollow cylinder with a student placed against the wall as shown. When the cylinder rotates quickly enough, the student is able to lift her feet off the floor and remain stuck to the wall. In terms of the student's mass \( m \), the radius of the cylinder \( r \), the coefficient of static friction \( \mu \) between the student and the wall, and fundamental quantities, determine the minimum rotational velocity \( \omega \) that the ride can have while still allowing the student to stick to the wall and not slide down.

title: Dataset Open Question
rewritten_question_text: A block of mass \(m\) slides down a plane inclined at an angle \(\theta\) with kinetic friction coefficient \(\mu_k\). What is the block's acceleration down the incline?

title: Dataset Open Question
rewritten_question_text: A mass with unknown weight \( W \) is suspended from cords as shown above. When the system is in static equilibrium, the tension in the horizontal cord is 10 N. What is the weight \( W \) of the mass?

title: Dataset Open Question
rewritten_question_text: For an ideal solenoid, the magnetic field inside the solenoid is proportional to what quantity?
expected_answer: The magnetic field inside the solenoid is proportional to \( I \).

title: Dataset Open Question
rewritten_question_text: An electron traveling in the negative-\( x \) direction passes through a magnetic field and experiences a magnetic force in the positive-\( y \) direction, as shown. In which direction could the magnetic field be oriented?

title: Dataset Open Question 2
rewritten_question_text: The instantaneous velocity and net acceleration for an object moving in a circular path are shown above. At this moment in time, is the object moving clockwise or counterclockwise?

title: Dataset Open Question
rewritten_question_text: An amusement park ride consists of a large spinning cylinder of radius \( r \) with rough walls. At a given angular velocity \( \omega \), the horizontal floor of the cylinder drops, but a rider of mass \( m \) remains safely pinned against the walls of the cylinder. What is the minimum coefficient of static friction \( \mu \) necessary for the rider to keep from sliding down the wall?

title: Dataset Open Question
rewritten_question_text: A disk of mass \( m \) is placed on a frictionless table and attached to a vertically hanging mass \( M \) by a string that passes through a frictionless opening in the surface. The disk moves in a circle of constant radius \( R \) with speed \( v \). How much work is done on the disk in one half revolution?

title: Dataset Open Question

Consider the counterclockwise direction to be positive. When the spokes are oriented as shown in the diagram, what is the net torque on the wheel due to the weight of the spokes?

expected_answer: The needle deflects to the left.

expected_answer: it would equal the potential difference across the 30 ohm resistor
